commit.message:
v2: Fix scraper extracting platform-level links instead of individual job URLs

Problem: v1's generic extractor grabbed navigation links ("产品与技术"),
category labels ("技术运营类"), and JD text fragments as job entries.
ByteDance returned 0 real jobs, Tencent had no URLs, MiniMax had 30+
duplicate fragments.

Fix:
- Add platform-specific extractors for Feishu, ByteDance, Tencent
- URL pattern filtering: only keep /position/{ID}, /job_detail/{ID} etc
- Click-through for SPA cards that use click handlers instead of <a> tags
- Auto-pagination (default 3 pages)
- Proper deduplication by URL or title
- Structured field extraction (title, location, department, jobType)

New difficulty section added to README (Difficulty #8).

commit.message:
Add git-based agentic tasks — AI clones repo, works, pushes feature branch

Users provide a repo URL + PAT token; Runner pre-clones the repo into the
sandbox, AI makes changes on a feature branch, commits and pushes. User
reviews the PR on GitHub.

Changes:
- db: allows_git_tasks column, git_repo_url/git_branch in tasks_log
- config: GIT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, GIT_SANDBOX_CLEANUP_DELAY_MS
- provider: supportsGitTasks(), getAvailableGitProvidersForModel()
- telegram: /git on|off command with i18n
- runner: pre-clone with credential store, git result extraction
- server: git validation, routing, event forwarding, result relay
- frontend: git toggle button, repo/token panel, result card with PR link
- model descriptions: mark Claude Sonnet/Opus as Git-capable

Security: token in encrypted blob only, never in prompt or process args,
credential file in sandbox (mode 0600), wiped with cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pipeline: release bead back to open when all providers are rate limited (Forge-rpc) (#33)

* fix: release bead back to open when all providers are rate limited

When pipeline.Run() exhausted all providers due to rate limiting the bead
remained stuck in_progress with no active worker, requiring manual cleanup.

Changes:
- pipeline.go: detect all-providers-rate-limited, run `bd update <id>
  --status=open` to release the bead, set Outcome.RateLimited = true
- config.go: add settings.rate_limit_backoff (default 5m) so users can
  tune how long between the release and the next retry poll
- daemon.go: when outcome.RateLimited is set, hold the activeBeads slot
  for the backoff duration (context-aware) before returning so the poller
  does not immediately re-dispatch the same bead

Bead: Forge-rpc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-rpc

- Use exec.CommandContext with 30s timeout for bead release to avoid
  indefinite blocking when bd update hangs
- Only set outcome.RateLimited when bead release succeeds; on failure,
  log a DB event and return a regular error so the failure is surfaced
- Add config tests for RateLimitBackoff default (5m), YAML parsing, and
  invalid value rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(pdf): phases plan d'action — float→table-cell (WeasyPrint overlap)

Les 3 boîtes de phases (Urgences/Corrections/Optimisations) se
chevauchaient dans le PDF (page 7) : la Phase 2 recouvrait Phase 1
et Phase 3 se retrouvait décalée.

Cause : .action-phase utilisait float:left alors que tout le reste
du template utilise display:table/table-cell. WeasyPrint ne gère pas
les floats de manière fiable en contexte paginé.

Fix : alignement avec le pattern du reste du template
  .action-phases : overflow:hidden → display:table + table-layout:fixed + border-spacing:5pt
  .action-phase  : float:left + width:32% + margin-right:2% → display:table-cell + vertical-align:top

commit.message:
refactor(makefile): standardize image build/push with dynamic tagging

Standardized Makefile for krkn-operator core project to support beta release:

Changes:
- Add dynamic registry variables (COMPONENT, REGISTRY, IMG_NAME)
- Add git tag detection (GIT_TAG) for automatic versioning
- Update docker-build to tag both :latest and :tag (if git tag exists)
- Update docker-push to push both :latest and :tag (if exists)
- Apply same logic to data-provider build/push targets
- Add NAMESPACE variable (default: krkn-operator-system)
- Update deploy to auto-create namespace if missing
- Update deploy to support namespace override
- Update deploy-openshift and undeploy to use NAMESPACE variable
- Maintain full Docker/Podman support (CONTAINER_TOOL)

Image tagging behavior:
- Without git tag: builds/pushes :latest only
- With git tag: builds/pushes both :latest and :<tag>

Override examples:
- make docker-build (uses latest)
- make docker-build IMG_TAG=v1.0.0 (custom tag)
- make deploy NAMESPACE=custom-ns (custom namespace)
- make podman-build (uses podman instead of docker)

All podman-* targets remain functional.

Part of: tsebastiani-ys6

commit.message:
fix: Interessensgebiet via Python + Transport Inland-Produkte

Interessensgebiet:
- Nicht mehr via Agent (unzuverlässig wegen API-Format-Unsicherheit)
- Stattdessen: Python _detect_interessensgebiet() nach Agent-Run
  → Keyword-Matching auf Deal-Titel + Kategorie → Option-ID String
  → Direkt pipedrive_update_deal() aufrufen, Format garantiert korrekt
  → Fallback: Webhook-Payload falls Agent-Kategorie leer

Transportkosten:
- System-Prompt komplett überarbeitet mit exakten Produktnamen:
  innerhalb Wien / kleiner 50km / kleiner 150km / größer 150km
- Distanzreferenz Wien→AT-Städte: Graz=200km, Linz=190km, etc.
- Explizites Verbot: NIEMALS „Speditionskosten Ausland"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: diagnostic provider for syntax error squiggles (#20)

## Summary

- Adds a \`DiagnosticCollection\` that surfaces ANTLR syntax errors as
red squiggles in \`.strm\` files
- Runs the full ANTLR lexer + parser on open/change (debounced 500 ms)
and clears diagnostics on close
- New \`getParseErrors()\` export in \`src/parser.ts\` — lexer errors
and parser errors are collected separately via a shared
\`SyntaxErrorCollector\` class extending \`antlr4.error.ErrorListener\`
- New file: \`src/diagnosticProvider.ts\` keeps event subscriptions and
debounce logic self-contained
- Regenerates all \`src/_js_parser/\` files from the current
\`process.g4\` grammar (ANTLR 4.12.0, JavaScript target) — the old
parser was generated from a stale grammar that was missing \`labelStmt\`
and \`modelStmt\`
- Fixes a webpack dev-build bug in \`processLexer.js\`: the generated
code imported \`isSQLStmt\` from \`processParser.js\`, making it a
read-only ES module binding; assigning to it in \`SQL_action\` silently
broke the parser in the dev bundle, causing \`getParseErrors\` to fail
on any \`.strm\` file containing a \`sql\` statement — replaced with a
local \`let isSQLStmt = false\` declaration
- Removes the Go-specific \`var isSQLStmt bool\` member that ANTLR
codegen left in the \`processParser.js\` constructor (invalid JS)
- Updates \`testdata/sample.strm\` to match grammar constraints: model
nodes have no name field, \`exec_when=\` is not a valid node option,
\`mode=\` is only valid on S3 output statements

## Test plan

- [x] Open \`testdata/sample.strm\` — Problems panel should be empty
(valid file)
- [x] Introduce a deliberate syntax error (e.g. \`nod filternode\`
instead of \`node\`) — red squiggle should appear within ~500 ms
- [x] Fix the error — squiggle should clear
- [x] Close the file — diagnostics entry should be removed from the
Problems panel
- [x] Verify no false positives on \`sample.strm\` (ANTLR error recovery
is permissive; some unusual constructs may still produce spurious errors
— document any found during review)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
